mission.title: kubernetes: Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec
mission.description: Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec. Requested by 426+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubernetes deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubernetes version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes
helm list -n kubernetes 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubernetes installation.

steps.title: Review kubernetes configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubernetes configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes
kubectl get configmap -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubernetes
```
It looks like `docker exec` is being used as the backend for `kubectl exec`. `docker exec` has the `--user` flag, which allows you to run a command as a particular user.  This same functionality doesn't exist in Kubernetes.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec
steps.description:
considerations:
* uid would need to be part of the exec interface for CRI, and all runtimes would need to support it
* would need to figure out what the authorization check would be w.r.t. pod security policy restrictions on uids

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes
kubectl get events -n kubernetes --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
considerations:
* uid would need to be part of the exec interface for CRI, and all runtimes would need to support it
* would need to figure out what the authorization check would be w.r.t. pod security policy restrictions on uids

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubernetes installed or the issue environment reproducible.
